column1: # ESP-025 bench rig partition table — classic ESP32 DevKit (WROOM-32)
# NOT the product table. partitions.csv is a 16 MB layout for the Waveshare S3 and is
# INVALID on this chip: its app0 alone is 0x400000 at offset 0x10000
# the end of a 4 MB part. The bootloader rejects the table outright and the board
# boot-loops with "partition 2 invalid ... exceeds flash chip size" -- no app
# banner
column2: nothing to debug against. Arduino gives a sketch exactly ONE partitions.csv

column1: # so the two boards cannot share it.
# Do not swap this in by hand -- use tools/build-bench.sh
# AND this table together and restores both on exit. Committing a half-swap breaks the
# product build.
# Dual-OTA so the bench rig can take a web/OTA push like the product does. 0x1A0000
# (1.625 MB) per slot vs a ~1.0 MB image = ~60% used
column2: room to grow. nvs and otadata sit

column1: # at the Arduino default offsets (0x9000 / 0xe000) so an OTA preserves saved WiFi/config.

column2: then spiffs + coredump end exactly at 4 MB.

column1: # Name
nvs
otadata
app0
app1
